summary: Comprehensive and well-structured contract with 30 testable criteria covering arena validation, difficulty parameter checks, report structure, debug UI, regression, and integration. Criteria are specific, thresholds are measurable, and coverage maps cleanly to the sprint plan features.

arena-enclosure.assessment: Good. C-01 tests runtime output (13 stages, arenaResult.enclosure with PASS/FAIL), C-02 verifies implementation via code review (4 walls + entry exclusion). Clear thresholds.

arena-width.assessment: Good. Specific threshold (>= 15 tiles), testable via console output. Measured value reported for verification.

arena-platforms.assessment: Good. Specific threshold (count >= 2), testable via console output.

arena-entry.assessment: Good. C-05 tests output, C-06 verifies implementation checks ground-level walkable entry. Consistent with prompt's left-side entry requirement.

difficulty-vulnerability.assessment: Good. C-07 checks runtime output per world, C-08 verifies exact difficulty table values. Allows both seconds and frames representation.

difficulty-hp.assessment: Good. C-09 checks output, C-10 verifies HP is read from actual game data (not hardcoded). Important correctness check.

difficulty-density.assessment: Good. C-12 specifies sliding window algorithm (30 tiles = 960px/32px). Implementation detail is appropriate for a code-review criterion.

difficulty-hazards.assessment: Good. C-14 specifies formula (hazard tiles / non-empty tiles * 100). Clear and verifiable.

difficulty-platforms.assessment: Good. C-16 specifies scanning algorithm (contiguous horizontal runs of standable tiles). Clear.

report-structure.assessment: Good. Verifies clean JSON structure with labeled categories (pathResult, arenaResult, difficultyResult), sub-checks with result+details, and aggregate counts.

debug-ui.assessment: Adequate. C-23 verifies design spec color compliance. C-21 and C-22 could be slightly more specific about layout expectations, but are testable by visual inspection or Playwright.

regression.assessment: Good. Covers sprint 13 API output (path results still present, 13 stages), performance (60fps), and UI (debug overlay D/Escape, title screen nav). C-25 and C-26 duplicate sprint 13 regression checks but that's acceptable redundancy.

integration.assessment: Good. Ensures new code is self-contained in validator.js. Prevents risky modifications to core gameplay files.

featureCoverage.sprintPlanFeature: F24: Playability Validator System (arena validation + difficulty parameters)
covered: Arena enclosure validation (4 walls + entry exclusion)
Arena entry accessibility check (ground-level, no wall-jump)
Difficulty table: boss vulnerability windows per world
Difficulty table: boss HP ranges per world
Difficulty table: enemy density per screen per world
Difficulty table: hazard tile percentage per world
Difficulty table: minimum safe platform width per world
Debug overlay displays arena and difficulty results
Aggregate counts across all check categories
notCovered: Boss X position clamping to arena bounds (user prompt mentions this under Boss Arena Validation, but it's runtime behavior rather than static level analysis — reasonable to defer to Sprint 15 as a fix rather than a validation check)
Boss reset to center on player death (same reasoning — runtime behavior, not static validation)
featureCoverage.coverageVerdict: Coverage is strong for static validation scope. The two uncovered items are runtime behavioral checks that belong in Sprint 15's boss rebalancing fixes rather than in a static level validator. The sprint notes explicitly state this sprint only adds validation/reporting.

regressionCoverage.assessment: Complete. All 13 prior sprints listed. Sprint 13 (path validation) is the direct dependency and has explicit in-contract regression criteria (C-25 through C-29).

outOfScope.assessment: Well-defined. Clearly separates validation (this sprint) from fixing (Sprint 15/16). Lists specific fix categories that are deferred.

concerns.description: 30 criteria is a large contract for a medium-complexity sprint. However, many criteria come in console/code-review pairs testing the same feature from different angles, so actual implementation scope is reasonable (5 arena checks + 5 difficulty checks + report structure + UI updates).
concerns.recommendation: No action needed — the criteria are well-decomposed and individually lightweight.

concerns.description: C-21 and C-22 (debug UI display of arena/difficulty results) use 'manual' testMethod with somewhat vague 'visible in the overlay' thresholds. Playwright screenshot comparison could be unreliable.
concerns.recommendation: During evaluation, supplement visual checks with code review of the overlay rendering function to verify the data is rendered. Acceptable as-is.

concerns.description: The notes correctly explain that FAIL results from the validator are expected and correct behavior. The evaluator must not penalize stages that fail validation — only verify that the validator accurately reports results.
concerns.recommendation: Evaluator should verify validator runs without errors and produces structured output, not that all stages PASS.
